source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Crane/Other Lifting

event.top_event: Hand laceration and finger fracture from pinch point during pump installation
actions_taken: IP reported incident to Operations Lead and Medic
Medic cleaned injury and applied tube gauze
Medic consulted with occupational doctor onshore
IP evacuated by helicopter for medical evaluation
X-rays performed revealing distal left index finger fracture
IP released to full duty after treatment
event.summary: On 13 September 2019 at 1530 hours, an injured person sustained a laceration and fracture to the left index finger while assisting with installation of a flow assurance pump on Deck 7 of the Gulfstar 1 platform. The injury occurred when an air-operated chain hoist jerked upward while the IP was using an alignment tool to position piping flanges, causing the IP's hand to be pinched between the piping and alignment bar. The incident was attributed to inadequate hazard analysis in the Job Safety Analysis, congested workspace, poor hand placement, and the jerking motion of the air hoist.
recommendations: Conduct and document Job Safety Analysis campaign with all personnel to include proper development, enhanced hazard recognition, and specific mitigations versus generalized/generic mitigations
Develop standard process for installation of vertical hull submersible pumps that directs the type of hoist and hand tools to use and how to secure O-Rings

hazards.name: Pinch point hazard in confined workspace
Congested work environment with multiple piping runs

threats.name: Uncontrolled jerking motion of air hoist during load adjustment
Inadequate recognition of specific pinch point hazard in JSA
Poor hand placement due to body position constraints in cramped workspace
Use of air hoist with single speed control instead of manual hoist

consequences.name: Fracture of distal left index finger
Medical evacuation and loss of work time

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and mitigations prior to work

supporting_text: Although the Job Safety Analysis (JSA) identified 'pinch points' as a work hazard for the job, this specific pinch point hazard was not recognized.
Management Systems - Inadequate hazard analysis: Although the Job Safety Analysis (JSA) identified 'pinch points' as a work hazard for the job, this specific pinch point hazard was not recognized.

controls.name: Air-operated chain hoist with load control
controls.barrier_role: Control mechanical energy and load movement during equipment installation
controls.lod_basis: Mechanical lifting device for load control

supporting_text: The air hoist has one speed to control the load's raising and lowering motion. Consequently, the speed of the air hoist caused the piping assembly to jerk upwards which led to the injury.
If the maintenance team had switched to a manual chain hoist, this would have allowed finer adjustments of equipment without quick movements possibly avoiding the injury.

controls.name: Workspace design and layout planning
controls.barrier_role: Minimize congestion and pinch point hazards in work area
controls.lod_basis: Facility design and work area organization

supporting_text: The work area was located in a cramped portion of the deck with three runs of piping of various sizes and several tubing runs. This made it difficult for equipment assembly without the use of mechanical lifting devices.
Work Environment- Congested hazardous workspace: The work area was located in a cramped portion of the deck with three runs of piping of various sizes and several tubing runs.

controls.name: Proper hand placement and body positioning during alignment work
controls.barrier_role: Prevent hand placement in pinch points during equipment manipulation

supporting_text: The body position of the IP holding the alignment bar led to poor hand placement.
Human error - Poor Hand Placement: The body position of the IP holding the alignment bar led to poor hand placement

controls.name: Immediate incident response and medical treatment
controls.barrier_role: Minimize injury severity through rapid medical intervention

supporting_text: The personnel present stopped the job and the IP reported the incident to the Operations Lead and Medic to seek medical treatment.

The Medic cleaned the injury and applied tube gauze to stabilize the injury. The Medic consulted with an occupational doctor onshore who directed that the IP should be flown in for further evaluation.
A Bristow helicopter was in the field at the time of the incident on the Motor Vessel Q4000. The Hess shore base contacted the helicopter to fly the IP in for further medical evaluation.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
